acceptance:
1. ddx install copies bin/* to ~/.local/bin/ when target is not a symlink
2. ddx install skips copy when target IS a symlink (prints notice)
3. ddx install overwrites stale file copies on update
4. ddx doctor reports launcher status: OK/STALE/MISSING with actionable message
5. ddx doctor distinguishes symlink (developer) from file copy (user)
description:
When a DDX plugin has a bin/ directory (e.g., helix), ddx install should:

1. Copy bin/* to ~/.local/bin/ (or the configured bin dir) — but ONLY if the target is NOT a symlink. If it's a symlink, the developer is managing it themselves (e.g., symlink to their git checkout).
2. On subsequent ddx install/update, overwrite the file copy if the plugin version changed — but still skip symlinks.
3. ddx doctor should check that ~/.local/bin/<name> exists and is either:
   - A symlink (developer mode — report OK, show target)
   - A file matching the installed plugin version (report OK)
   - A file NOT matching the installed plugin version (report STALE, suggest ddx install)
   - Missing (report MISSING, suggest ddx install)

This enables the pattern where:
- Developers: ln -s ~/Projects/helix/scripts/helix ~/.local/bin/helix
- Users: ddx install helix copies scripts/helix to ~/.local/bin/helix
- Updates: ddx install helix overwrites the copy but respects the symlink
title: ddx install: manage ~/.local/bin launcher for plugins with bin/

acceptance: ddx exec run output is condensed by default, filtering raw diffs and verbose output while preserving progress lines, tool calls, errors, markdown structure, and issue IDs. Full raw output preserved in execution run record. Filter rules configurable via caller namespace prefix.
description:
When ddx dispatches an agent via ddx exec run, raw agent output (especially codex) can be 200K+ lines per cycle — mostly raw file diffs. DDx should own output filtering since it owns agent dispatch. Add condensing to ddx exec run that filters agent stdout before returning to caller while preserving full output in the execution run record.

FILTER RULES — Keep: caller namespace lines (e.g. helix:), tool calls (lines starting with $), errors/warnings, issue/artifact IDs (hx-*, FEAT-*, US-*, commit SHA), markdown structure (#, |, **), phase markers (Phase, Step, ---), status keywords (COMPLETE/BLOCKED/CLOSED). Drop: raw diffs (diff --, @@, +/- content), codex boilerplate (Commands run:, tokens used), bulk blank lines, all other verbose output.

EXAMPLE — 34-line input with diffs and verbose output condenses to 10 progress-relevant lines. Full raw output stays in execution run record for debugging.

IMPLEMENTATION — Filter should be a reusable function in the agent package. Caller namespace prefix configurable. Token extraction must happen before condensing (reads raw output).
title: Add agent output condensing filter to ddx exec

acceptance:
1. Virtual agent can match prompts with dynamic paths to recorded responses
2. Normalization patterns configurable in .ddx/config.yaml
3. ddx agent run --record normalizes before storing
4. ddx agent run --harness virtual normalizes before lookup
description:
The virtual agent uses exact SHA-256 matching on prompt text. This prevents use in test harnesses where prompts contain dynamic content (temp directory paths, timestamps, issue IDs).

HELIX tests generate prompts with dynamic paths like /tmp/tmp.ABC123/work that change every run, breaking the hash match.

Proposed: add configurable normalization patterns (regex→replacement) applied before hashing. Config in .ddx/config.yaml:
  agent:
    virtual:
      normalize:
        - pattern: '/tmp/[a-zA-Z0-9._]+' 
          replace: '<TMPDIR>'
        - pattern: 'hx-[a-f0-9]+'
          replace: '<BEAD_ID>'

Both --record and --harness virtual apply the same normalization before hashing, ensuring test prompts match recorded responses regardless of dynamic content.
title: Virtual agent: support prompt normalization for test harnesses

acceptance: Run helix run --agent claude --summary --max-cycles 3 against a repo with 3 ready beads. After completion: all 3 beads remain closed (ddx bead list shows closed). No bead reverts to open. The blocker report shows 0 intractable issues.
description: The helix run worker closes beads via the agent, but between cycles the beads revert from closed to open. The chaos tests (cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go) prove the JSONL store locking is sound under concurrent access. The bug is in helix run's cycle loop: after the agent closes a bead and commits, the worker's next cycle startup reclaims or reopens the bead. Likely cause: helix run reads the bead list at cycle start, caches it, then writes back the stale cached state after the agent has already modified beads.jsonl. Root cause is in ~/Projects/helix/scripts/helix (the helix run bash script), not in ddx. Fix should ensure helix run re-reads beads.jsonl fresh after each agent call returns, never caching stale state across cycle boundaries.
title: Fix helix worker bead-state-drift: closed beads revert to open between cycles

acceptance:
1. ddx agent run --harness virtual replays from dictionary file
2. ddx agent record --harness claude captures prompt→response pairs
3. Pattern matching finds the best response for a given prompt
4. Configurable delay for realistic timing in recordings
5. default_agent: virtual in config makes it the default
6. Works with both helix and ddx demo scripts
description:
Add a 'virtual' agent to ddx agent that replays canned prompt→response pairs from a dictionary file instead of calling a real LLM API.

Use case:
- Deterministic demo recordings (no API key needed, consistent output)
- CI testing of workflows that exercise agent calls
- Fast iteration on demo scripts without burning tokens
- Both helix and ddx can use this for their demo reels

The dictionary maps prompt patterns (regex or substring match) to response text. When ddx agent run --harness virtual receives a prompt, it looks up the best match and returns the canned response with realistic timing.

Dictionary format (YAML or JSONL):
  - pattern: 'Create the Frame-phase artifacts'
    response: |
      Created docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md
      Created docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
      ...
    delay_ms: 5000

Generation: ddx agent record --harness claude captures real prompt→response pairs into the dictionary. ddx agent replay --harness virtual replays them.

Config: set default_agent: virtual in .ddx/config.yaml to use virtual agent everywhere without changing scripts.
title: agent: add virtual agent harness for deterministic replay

acceptance: The DDx tracker contains dependency-gated child tasks that implement the generic `ddx exec` substrate, layer metric-specific behavior on top without reintroducing a separate metric store, and remediate migration/API/docs/test fallout. Each child references the governing execution and metric artifacts, names concrete tests, and supersedes the earlier closed metric-only implementation plan.
description: Background: DDx frame and design docs now define a generic execution substrate (`FEAT-010`, `SD-005`, `TD-005`, `TP-005`) where artifacts stay declarative and runtime evidence lives in `ddx exec` definitions and runs. The code in the current worktree has not been reconciled to that model: it still exposes a metric-specific CLI and store (`cli/cmd/metric.go`, `cli/internal/metric/*`) and still writes to `.ddx/metrics/*`. That means the documented abstraction and the implementation target have diverged. This epic replaces the earlier closed metric backlog slice as the actionable implementation contract. Scope this tranche to: (1) implementing the generic execution substrate, (2) re-layering metrics as a specialization over that substrate, and (3) remediating storage, API, migration, tests, and docs so the codebase and specs agree. Require each child task to preserve artifact-generic boundaries, generate deterministic tests, and state any compatibility behavior explicitly.
title: Reconcile metric implementation with the DDx execution model
